items.label: Anger / Outrage
items.definition: Hot affect produced by perceived violation, injustice, betrayal, or attack on self, ingroup, or core values. Mobilizes confrontation, public denunciation, and protest behavior.

anchors.0: No anger; accepts or ignores the issue without irritation.
anchors.3: Mild irritation or grumbling complaint; would tell a friend but not post.
anchors.5: Clear anger and explicit criticism; willing to speak publicly and challenge.
anchors.8: Strong outrage with confrontational language and demands for accountability.
anchors.10: Extreme outrage; affect is highly escalatory and crowds out other reasoning.

items.label: Fear / Threat Perception
items.definition: Anticipatory anxiety about personal, family, or in-group harm, including economic, legal, physical, or status threat. Drives avoidance, withdrawal, and protective coordination.

anchors.0: No sense of threat; situation feels safe and bounded.
anchors.3: Background unease; checks news more often, hedges plans.
anchors.5: Clear worry; takes concrete precautions and discusses with trusted contacts.
anchors.8: Acute fear; reorganizes daily routines, may stockpile or self-isolate.
anchors.10: Panic-level fear; behavior dominated by avoidance and threat scanning.

items.label: Distrust
items.definition: Default skepticism toward an actor, institution, or information source; reduces persuasive weight of their statements regardless of content.

anchors.0: Full default credit extended; assumes good faith.
anchors.5: Clear distrust; assumes motivated framing and discounts heavily.
anchors.8: Strong distrust; assumes deception or capture by hostile interests.
anchors.10: Total distrust; treats all statements as adversarial signaling.

items.label: Trust
items.definition: Default willingness to extend credit to an actor, institution, or information source; raises persuasive weight of their statements and lowers verification cost.

anchors.0: No trust extended; treats source as adversarial.
anchors.3: Limited trust; accepts narrow factual claims, doubts framing.
anchors.5: General trust; accepts most claims with normal verification.
anchors.8: Strong trust; accepts framing and recommendations with little independent check.
anchors.10: Unconditional trust; defers fully to the source's interpretation.

items.label: Hope
items.definition: Forward-looking belief that conditions can improve and that one's actions or those of allies can affect outcomes. Sustains engagement against setbacks.

anchors.0: No hope; outcome is fixed and bad.
anchors.3: Faint hope; concedes change is possible but unlikely.
anchors.5: Active hope; believes meaningful progress is realistic with effort.
anchors.8: Strong hope; expects significant positive change and recruits others.
anchors.10: Exuberant hope; conviction that breakthrough is imminent.

items.label: Calm
items.definition: Regulated, low-arousal affect that allows analytical engagement, patience, and de-escalation. Inverse of emotional flooding.

anchors.0: No calm; flooded by other strong affect.
anchors.3: Mostly agitated but with brief composed moments.
anchors.5: Generally composed; can deliberate without reactivity.
anchors.8: Steady and grounded; visibly de-escalates others.
anchors.10: Deeply settled; unshakable composure under pressure.

items.label: Confusion
items.definition: Subjective uncertainty about what is happening, who to believe, or what to do. Suppresses confident action and increases search behavior.

anchors.0: Situation is fully legible; high subjective clarity.
anchors.3: Some open questions; broadly understands the picture.
anchors.5: Clear confusion; conflicting accounts make action choice hard.
anchors.8: Strong confusion; cannot evaluate basic claims or sources.
anchors.10: Total disorientation; gives up evaluating and defers to in-group cues.

items.label: Urgency
items.definition: Felt pressure that action must happen soon. Compresses deliberation windows and biases toward immediate posting, mobilization, or scheduling.

anchors.0: No urgency; treats issue as distant or chronic.
anchors.3: Mild urgency; intends to act this week.
anchors.5: Clear urgency; expects to act within days, sets reminders.
anchors.8: Strong urgency; rearranges schedule today to act.
anchors.10: Extreme urgency; drops other commitments to act now.

items.label: Sympathy
items.definition: Concern-oriented affect toward identifiable victims or affected parties; raises willingness to support, donate, share, or absorb cost on their behalf.

anchors.3: Mild concern; acknowledges harm but does not act.
anchors.5: Clear sympathy; shares stories, signals support.
anchors.8: Strong sympathy; volunteers time, donates, advocates publicly.
anchors.10: Profound sympathy; reorients personal priorities around the affected.

items.label: Disgust
items.definition: Moral or visceral rejection of an actor, behavior, or symbol perceived as contaminating or violating sacred norms. Drives boycott, exclusion, and severance.

anchors.0: No disgust; behavior is acceptable or neutral.
anchors.3: Mild distaste; would prefer to avoid.
anchors.5: Clear disgust; refuses association and signals condemnation.
anchors.8: Strong disgust; calls for exclusion or boycott of associated parties.
anchors.10: Visceral revulsion; treats target as illegitimate, beyond dialogue.

items.label: Resentment
items.definition: Slow-burning grievance accumulated over perceived unfair treatment, status loss, or repeated dismissal. Lowers willingness to cooperate even when interests align.

anchors.5: Clear resentment; selectively withholds cooperation, keeps score.
anchors.8: Strong resentment; actively frustrates the resented party where low-cost.
anchors.10: Deep, identity-defining resentment; will absorb cost to deny the other a win.

items.label: Sadness
items.definition: Low-arousal grief or demoralization in response to loss, defeat, or bleak prospects. Reduces public expression and mobilization in the short term.

anchors.0: No sadness; affect is neutral or positive.
anchors.3: Mild low mood; less talkative, slower to share.
anchors.5: Clear sadness; visibly subdued, withdraws from public discussion.
anchors.8: Strong sadness; demoralized, doubts efficacy of any action.
anchors.10: Deep grief or despair; effectively unable to mobilize without recovery.
